commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a memory-wiki bridge fallback that deep-imports memory-core public artifacts, and also includes a separate skills wizard empty-state note.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, but not from an executed current-main scenario: the wiki bridge/status path ultimately depends on `listActiveMemoryPublicArtifacts`, which returns empty when no memory capability has registered. The linked issue supplies a concrete config and observed zero-artifact output, but this review did not run the live CLI flow.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action is needed for a contract-safe implementation and real behavior proof, so this should not receive an automated repair marker yet.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): add fallback to direct memory-core import when capability not registered
target.body:
Fixes #85655

## Root Cause

listActiveMemoryPublicArtifacts depends on global memoryPluginState.capability. In CLI context or early gateway initialization, memory plugin capability may not be registered yet, causing wiki bridge import/status to report 0 artifacts.

## Fix

Add fallback to directly import memory-core listMemoryCorePublicArtifacts when:
- Registered capability returns empty
- Memory slot is configured as memory-core

## Test Plan

- Unit tests: 13 tests passed

## Security Checklist

- No new external dependencies
- Dynamic import only from bundled plugin
- Fallback gracefully handles import failures
